page.content: The first table presents EEG measures and their corresponding clinical scales, featuring headers for EEG measure and clinical scale. The ITC chirp shows a sensory profile—auditory of NS, a SCQ total score of −.74*, and a deviation score for nonverbal IQ of NS. The STP gamma indicates a sensory profile—auditory of .61*, a SCQ total score of .84**, and a deviation score for nonverbal IQ of NS. The pre-stimulus theta/gamma amp-amp coupling has a sensory profile—auditory of NS, a SCQ total score of .74*, and a deviation score for nonverbal IQ of NS. Lastly, the pre-stimulus theta/gamma phase amplitude coupling—vector length presents a sensory profile—auditory of NS, a SCQ total score of NS, and a deviation score for nonverbal IQ of .58*.

page.content: The second table outlines variables related to health outcomes, with headers for variables, GP visits, hospital admission, and new health problem. The age variable has a GP visits correlation of −0.175*, a hospital admission correlation of 0.002, and a new health problem correlation of 0.149. Gender shows a GP visits correlation of 0.096, a hospital admission correlation of 0.074, and a new health problem correlation of −0.022. The marital status variable correlates with GP visits at −0.076, hospital admission at 0.053, and new health problem at −0.114. Urban/rural living has correlations of −0.019 for GP visits, 0.031 for hospital admission, and −0.145 for new health problem. The GFI score correlates with GP visits at −0.076, hospital admission at 0.146*, and new health problem at 0.107. The adjusted R squared values are 0.043 for GP visits, 0.002 for hospital admission, and 0.027 for new health problem.
